commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a terminal `ci-timings-summary` CI job that writes `scripts/ci-run-timings.mjs` output to the step summary and uploads it as an artifact, with matching docs and workflow guard coverage.

PR surface: Tests +43, Docs 0, Config +50. Total +93 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction path; this is a CI automation restoration PR. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the artifact job and the PR adds it.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide and fix the trusted-code boundary for the new `GH_TOKEN` workflow path before merge; the PR also has the protected maintainer label.

target.title: ci: restore timing summary artifact
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore the terminal `ci-timings-summary` CI job.
- Write the current run timing summary to `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` and upload it as a `ci-timings-summary` artifact.
- Add a workflow guard for the timing-summary job and update `docs/ci.md` to describe the restored artifact.


## Background

The `ci-timings-summary` job was added in `c3853611ee` (`ci: add runner fallback timing telemetry`) and later disappeared in `330ba1fa31` (`refactor: move canvas to plugin surfaces`), via #78558. That removal happened inside a broad Canvas/plugin-surface refactor; I did not find PR body or review discussion indicating a deliberate reason to drop the timing artifact.

Restoring the artifact gives maintainers a durable, compact run summary for wall time, queue time, slowest jobs, and failures without repeatedly scraping the full Actions payload. Keeping the same text in `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` also makes the data visible directly from the run page.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:workflows`
- `node` workflow-shape assertion for `ci-timings-summary` permissions, `needs`, summary write step, and artifact upload step
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check .github/workflows/ci.yml docs/ci.md test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: CI now publishes the run timing summary as a durable `ci-timings-summary` artifact again, while still appending the same summary to the Actions step summary.

Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on branch `codex/restore-ci-timings-artifact`, rebased from current `origin/main` after #87813 merged.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran workflow parsing/shape checks, docs checks, formatting check, whitespace check, and autoreview locally.

Evidence after fix: Parsed `.github/workflows/ci.yml` contains a terminal `ci-timings-summary` job with `actions: read`, current CI lane `needs`, `node scripts/ci-run-timings.mjs "$GITHUB_RUN_ID" --limit 25`, `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` output, and an `actions/upload-artifact@v7` upload named `ci-timings-summary`.

Observed result after fix: Workflow/docs checks passed and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: A live GitHub Actions run of the new job. Local Vitest via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts` hung without output in this Codex worktree twice, so I stopped those local Vitest processes and used the direct workflow-shape assertion instead.
target.author: romneyda
